author: time news
title: Numerous evidence points against Bolsonaro for attempted coup and assassination against Lula
text:
The ‚ÄčFederal Police of‚Ā§ Brazil is investigating Jair Bolsonaro and ‚Ā£36 other ‚Äčpeople ‚Äúviolent abolition of the democratic rule of law, coup d‚Äô√©tat‚ĀĘ and illicit association‚ÄĚ . The investigations focus on a possible plot to prevent Luiz In√°cio‚Ā§ Lula da Silva from assuming the presidency.
The investigation, which covers a broad spectrum of actions and meetings, has gathered six ‚Ā£key pieces of evidence linking the former president to the plan. It ‚ÄĆshould be remembered ‚Ā£that the assault on the Plaza de los Tres Poderes in Brasilia took place on ‚ĀĘJanuary 8, 2023. when supporters of far-right former president‚Äć Jair Bolsonaro‚ĀĘ invaded the buildings of Brazil‚Äôs three powers‚ÄĆ in an attempt to overthrow the then-president-elect. Luiz In√°cio Lula Da‚ĀĘ Silva .
Meetings with the‚Äč armed ‚ĀĘforces Prominent among the evidence are Bolsonaro‚Äôs ‚Ā£meetings with ‚Ā§senior military commanders. In a meeting at Alvorada‚ÄĆ Palace on December 7, 2022, he ‚ÄĆpresented a‚ÄĆ plan to declare a state of siege and create a commission ‚Äćto investigate the 2022 elections.
Generals Marco ‚ÄčAnt√īnio Freire Gomes and Carlos de ‚ÄčAlmeida Baptista J√ļnior confirmed the existence of this proposal, presented a few days after Bolsonaro‚Äôs electoral ‚ÄĆdefeat. The investigation analyzes the nature and extent of these conversations, as well as the possible ‚Ā§role of the military in the plan.
Compromising documents found Draft decrees were found in the home of the‚Äć former Minister of Justice, Anderson ‚ÄĆTorres. These documents,‚Äč some dated 2022‚Ā£ and mentioning Bolsonaro, raised interventions‚Äč at the Superior Electoral Tribunal (TSE) and the possibility of new elections.
Although they were ‚ÄĆnot signed or made ‚ÄĆofficial, the investigation‚Ā§ suggests that Bolsonaro reviewed them and‚ÄĆ requested changes before leaving office. Its content and the extent ‚Äćof the former president‚Äôs participation in its ‚Ā§preparation‚Äč are analyzed.
The ‚ĀĘinvestigation also reveals a document titled ‚ÄúGreen-Yellow Dagger‚ÄĚ, which details a plan to assassinate key figures such as Lula da Silva, ‚ÄĆGeraldo Alckmin and‚Ā£ Alexandre de Moraes. The monitoring ‚Ā§of‚Äč Moraes, key to ‚ÄĆelectoral supervision, would be directed by M√°rio Fernandes, with ‚Ā£the participation of Lieutenant Colonel Mauro Cid. The federal police believe the aim‚Ā§ was‚Äč to kidnap ‚Ā§and kill the magistrate. The chain of command and the feasibility of the plan are examined.
Print documents at Palazzo Planalto The documents‚Ā§ relating to the ‚ÄúGreen-Yellow Dagger‚ÄĚ project were printed in‚Äč the Planalto Palace. using ‚ĀĘstate resources. General Mario Fernandes made six copies on December 16, 2022, ‚ĀĘwhich were ‚ÄĆthen ‚Äčdistributed during private meetings. The next ‚ÄĆday he visited the ‚Ā§Alvorada Palace, presumably to present the material to Bolsonaro and other aides. This reveals the use of public resources for the conspiracy.
Before the elections, Bolsonaro met‚ÄĆ with members of his cabinet at‚Ā£ the Planalto Palace to discuss strategies ‚ÄĆin case of defeat. Augusto Heleno, head of ‚ĀĘthe Office for Institutional‚ĀĘ Security, suggested ‚ÄĆ‚ÄĚchanging the ‚Ā§situation‚ÄĚ before the elections. M√°rio Fernandes proposed ‚Äúa small risk of disorganization of the country‚ÄĚ if intervention in electoral supervision was not allowed. It is being ‚Äčinvestigated whether ‚ĀĘthese proposals constituted a premeditated plan.
Using the Liberal Party to spread conspiracy theories The‚Ā§ Liberal Party (PL), led‚Ā£ by Bolsonaro, has spread stories of electoral fraud. These allegations culminated in ‚Ā£a lawsuit to‚ĀĘ invalidate the election results.
Bolsonaro‚Äôs electoral ‚Ā£committee was used to promote actions that‚ÄĆ encouraged military intervention. The role of the party in spreading ‚Ā£disinformation and‚Äć attempting‚Äč to delegitimize the electoral process is studied.
What are the primary legal challenges Jair Bolsonaro is facing ‚Äčin the ongoing investigation? Interview: Unpacking the Investigation‚ĀĘ into Jair Bolsonaro
Editor of Time.news: Welcome to‚Äč today‚Äôs discussion. ‚ÄćWe‚Äôre here with Dr.‚Äć Mariana Santos, a political analyst and expert on Brazilian politics, to ‚ÄĆdelve into the ongoing investigation involving former‚Äć President Jair Bolsonaro and several others. Dr. Santos, it‚Äôs great to have you here.
Dr. Mariana Santos: Thank you for having me. It‚Äôs‚Ā£ a crucial ‚Ā£time for Brazil‚Äôs democracy, and I‚Äôm‚ÄĆ glad to discuss it.
Editor: Let‚Äôs dive right in. The Federal Police‚ĀĘ of ‚ÄĆBrazil are‚ĀĘ investigating Bolsonaro‚Äč and 36 others for what they describe as‚Ā§ ‚Äúviolent abolition of the‚Ā§ democratic ‚Äćrule of law.‚ÄĚ Can you give us a‚Ā£ bit of background on the events leading up to this investigation?
Dr. Santos: Certainly. The investigation primarily stems ‚ĀĘfrom the ‚ĀĘJanuary 8, 2023, assault on the Plaza de los Tres Poderes in Bras√≠lia, where Bolsonaro‚Äôs supporters invaded ‚ĀĘgovernment buildings in an attempt to overturn the election results that favored Luiz In√°cio Lula da Silva. This‚Ā£ attack raised alarming concerns about the stability of Brazil‚Äôs democracy and led to scrutiny of Bolsonaro‚Äôs actions‚Ā£ and communications leading up to it.
Editor: Among the‚Äć evidence, investigations‚ÄĆ highlight meetings between‚ĀĘ Bolsonaro and high-ranking military officials. What‚ÄĆ do these meetings indicate about the military‚Äôs involvement or stance during this‚ÄĆ period?
Dr. Santos: The meetings, particularly ‚Äćone held on December 7, 2022, at the Alvorada Palace, are quite telling. Bolsonaro allegedly ‚Äćpresented a plan ‚ĀĘto declare a state of siege, proposing to investigate the 2022 elections under the justification of alleged irregularities. The involvement ‚Ā§of military generals confirms that there was considerable discussion and possibly a coordinated‚Äč effort to undermine the electoral outcomes. This is concerning because it blurs the lines between civilian ‚ÄĆgovernance ‚Äčand military involvement in politics.
Editor: There have ‚Äćbeen reports of compromising documents‚Äč found in the home of former Minister of Justice Anderson Torres. Can‚Äč you explain what ‚ÄĆthese documents entail, and why‚Ā§ they are particularly ‚ÄĆsignificant to this investigation?
Dr. ‚ÄĆSantos: Yes, the documents include‚Ā§ draft decrees that seem ‚Äčto outline‚ÄĆ interventions at Brazil‚Äôs Superior Electoral Tribunal (TSE) and even‚Äć suggest the possibility of holding new elections. Although these drafts weren‚Äôt signed, the investigation is examining their content closely, especially since they indicate that Bolsonaro ‚Ā§may have reviewed ‚ÄĆand requested amendments before leaving office. This further suggests a premeditated attempt to challenge ‚Äćand‚Ā£ potentially overturn the electoral process.
Editor: ‚ĀĘAdditionally, there‚Äôs mention of a disturbing document called the ‚ÄúGreen-Yellow Dagger,‚ÄĚ which allegedly details assassination plans against key political figures. What implications does this hold for Brazil‚Äôs political climate?
Dr. Santos: The existence of such ‚ÄĆa document is deeply troubling and signals a dangerous escalation in‚Äč political extremism. It suggests that there were plans not ‚ĀĘjust to undermine the ‚ÄĆdemocratic process but to eliminate those who were‚Äć pivotal in that process‚ÄĒfigures like Lula, former‚ÄĆ Vice President Geraldo Alckmin, and Justice Alexandre ‚ĀĘde Moraes. Such threats can instill fear and division within society, making it harder ‚Ā§for Brazil to move forward in a ‚ÄĆunited manner. The investigation into this document reflects‚Äč a critical need‚Ā§ to address both ‚ÄĆpolitical violence and ‚ÄĆthreats to public figures in order ‚Äćto safeguard democratic‚Ā£ integrity.
Editor: With the breadth of this investigation, what do you think‚Äć the potential outcomes could be for Bolsonaro and the others implicated?
Dr.‚Ā§ Santos: The outcomes could range widely.‚Ā£ If found‚Äć guilty, Bolsonaro could face‚ÄĆ significant legal ramifications, including imprisonment. More profoundly, however, this investigation could initiate a broader reflection‚ÄĆ on democratic‚Ā£ values in Brazil and ‚ĀĘpotentially lead ‚ĀĘto reforms that strengthen institutions. Regardless of the individual consequences for Bolsonaro, the investigation ‚Äčunderscores a pivotal moment‚Äć for Brazilian democracy, where citizens must confront the challenges posed by anti-democratic sentiments.
Editor: Thank you, Dr. Santos, for ‚ĀĘyour insights on these pressing issues. It will‚Ā§ be crucial to see‚Ā£ how‚Äč this investigation unfolds and its impact on Brazil‚Äôs future.
Dr. Santos: Thank you for having me. It‚Äôs essential that we keep the conversation going, as ‚Äćthe‚Äć stakes for democracy are incredibly high.
